title: Western disturbance to bring rain in Gujarat; IMD predicts thunderstorms in several districts - GujaratSamachar English
full_text:
Western disturbance to bring rain in Gujarat; IMD predicts thunderstorms in several districts
Amid scorching heatwave conditions gripping Gujarat and several parts of India, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast a major weather change that could bring much-needed relief over the next two days.
Due to the activation of a Western Disturbance, parts of Saurashtra and South Gujarat are likely to witness light rain accompanied by thunderstorms and gusty winds, as per reports.
According to the IMD’s latest update, pre-monsoon activity is expected to trigger light to moderate rainfall and scattered showers across parts of Gujarat on May 22 and 23.
The coastal belt of Saurashtra is likely to experience a change in weather from Thursday itself. Districts including Amreli and Gir Somnath may receive rainfall starting today, while Bhavnagar is expected to witness light showers on Friday.
Scattered rain has also been predicted in the Union Territory of Diu. Several districts in South Gujarat are likely to remain cloudy with chances of light rain during the next two days.
While Gujarat prepares for a rainy spell, northern and eastern parts of the country continue to battle extreme heat. Delhi recorded its hottest May night so far this season, highlighting the intensity of the ongoing heatwave across the region.
Meanwhile, reports suggest that a strong Western Disturbance is rapidly advancing towards North India and is expected to impact more than six states in the coming days. Jammu and Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, and western Uttar Pradesh are all likely to witness changing weather conditions due to the system.
The IMD has forecast widespread rainfall activity across these regions, along with strong winds, dust storms, thunderstorms, and possible hailstorms in some hilly areas. Although the exact intensity of the rainfall remains uncertain, the weather system is expected to bring down temperatures significantly over the next 48 hours, offering partial relief from the severe summer heat.
